steps.title: Set the sentence boundary
steps.content: The blank follows the noun phrase "differences in" and introduces an explanation of those differences. The sentence needs punctuation strong enough to mark that boundary, plus a word that correctly links the two contrasting descriptions of 39P/Oterma and 83982 Crantor.

steps.title: Check contrast and punctuation
steps.content: Choice B leaves two independent clauses joined only by a comma after "active centaur." Choice C uses a comma where the sentence needs a stronger break after "behavior." Choice D creates the same comma-splice problem after the new sentence begins. A colon followed by "while" correctly introduces and contrasts the two descriptions. The answer is A.
